entry.title: Re: Cannot save more than 1 record in tablecontrol
entry.content: It's impossible to help without seeing your custom code.

entry.title: Re: How to Install an SSL/TLS Certificate on SAP Web Application Server?
entry.content:
Hi Tom,

You can use SAP CLM to automate the cert provisioning.

Here is the SAP Help link:

3088125 - How to configure Certificate Lifecycle Management using SAP Single Sign-On 3.0

entry.title: userexit_check_vbap process twice
entry.content:
Hi All,

I hope when user press Enter, then check some condition.

I wrote logic in userexit_check_vbap, when I debug code, it's process the logic twice.

How can I do that it trigger only one time

Please Help, Thanks all!

entry.title: Re: Cannot save more than 1 record in tablecontrol
entry.content:
IF sy-dynnr = '0014' AND
     ( sy-tcode = 'ME51N' OR
       sy-tcode = 'ME52N' OR
       sy-tcode = 'ME53N' OR
       sy-tcode = 'ME54N' ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'ZPR_HEADER_CUSTOM_SAVE'
      EXPORTING
        im_banfn = im_banfn_new.
  ENDIF.

FUNCTION zpr_header_custom_save.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IM_BANFN) TYPE  BANFN
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
  DATA: l_changed TYPE flag.
  DATA: l_count TYPE i.

  SELECT COUNT( * ) INTO l_count
         FROM eban
         WHERE banfn = im_banfn.
  IF l_count >= 1.
*    not create mode
*    we need to check if data has been changed
    CALL FUNCTION 'ZPR_CHECK_TRANSACTION_STATE'
      EXPORTING
        im_banfn   = im_banfn
      CHANGING
        ch_changed = l_changed.
    CHECK l_changed IS NOT INITIAL.
  ENDIF.
  LOOP AT g_zpaymentterm_itab INTO g_zpaymentterm_wa.
    g_zpaymentterm_wa-banfn = im_banfn.
    "MODIFY g_zpaymentterm_itab FROM g_zpaymentterm_wa TRANSPORTING banfn.

  CALL FUNCTION 'ZPR_HEADER_CUSTOM_SAVE_CORE' IN UPDATE TASK
    EXPORTING
      im_s_header = g_zpaymentterm_wa.
  ENDLOOP.

ENDFUNCTION.
FUNCTION zpr_header_custom_save_core.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Update Function Module:
*"
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(IM_S_HEADER) TYPE  ZPR_PAYTERM
*"  TABLES
*"      IM_S_HEADER_T STRUCTURE  ZPR_PAYTERM
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

*  MODIFY zpr_payterm FROM TABLE im_s_header_t.
  "MODIFY zpr_payterm FROM im_s_header.
  INSERT zpr_payterm FROM im_s_header.

ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_winkler,

Thanks for this. Just to clarify, what is the generated transport object mentioned in step 2 of the SAP S/4 Hana private cloud edition?

Thanks.

entry.title: How to make sales order mandatory for a production order type?
Re: Is there a T-Code / Process knowledge & Training data...
entry.content:
Hi Margit,

There is a clear need for a knowledge base by function/business competence versus which t-codes are applicable and how to work with them + how it all fits in the complete ERP package.

I Think the SAP Company should have a role in building such data knowledgebase as SAP is the creator of it's SAP Programs and should know the logic/structure/dependancies and how to work with each field and t-code better than it's end users. 

What would be your proposal to get something like this in place?

entry.title: Re: How to refresh currentUser after Google Oauth2 sign-in?
entry.content:
Hi!
Do you have any update on a potential fix for currentUser (firebase auth) flow function, so that it is forced to update the status based on the Firebase admin SDK?

Miguel Angel.

entry.title: Re: Enhance 0CO_OM_CCA_1 with COSP and COSS fields ?
entry.content:
Hello Romil,

how exactly did you append the field OBJNR to the structure of the data source 0CO_OM_CCA_1?
Did you append only the field or did you append a full (an new) structure?

We are facing a similar problem trying to append the field VRGNG to the structure, but it is not allowed to add the field, because it is already included in ICCTRCST_D.

Regards, Markus

entry.title: Re: Setting Default Values for Create in ABAP RESTful app...
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for sharing. What is the solution for onprem on lower versions ? When trying to use this feature we get an error ' "association create / default function" is not supported in this ABAP release. ' . This feels like such a basic requirement. Is there any alternative to default values of a child entity? Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Update custom fields in MARC - BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA
entry.content:
Hi,

I have the same problem and resolved it. We also need to maintain the added field in OMSR tcode or t130f table (SM30).

entry.title: Re: Cross Docking Process (WH -S/4 Cloud, Public Edition)
entry.content: Hi Geovana, my question is the same - is the CD available for Public Cloud edition? In system comparison (private - public) I can see only available option is the "Preallocated stock supported" But didnt find information what it means exactly. You mentioned about option with interimidate WH solution only. Does it mean CD doesnt work in public cloud for standard Warehouse? If its work, how is limited? For standard EWM I found some article where user described we need to activates two BADIs but I didnt found them in Public Cloud IMG..... Thank you

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome!
entry.content: Great to have it again this year 🙂

entry.title: Re: Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via APIs
entry.content:
Hi Mustafa/Ashish,

The communication management framework is available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: SAP Cloud studio - Unattended Bot - Execution after SAP logon System - login success
entry.content:
I am in the process of migrating all my unattended bots that were previously developed using Desktop Studio to Cloud studio. As of now I am done with a couple of bots and have checked their execution on my local machine in unattended mode, Everything works as expected.
However, when I schedule the same on the server machine that we have, which already has had unattended automations running since past few years. My newly built automations from the cloud studio get stuck after logging in to the SAP Systems via SAP Logon.
Local Desktop Agent Version : 2.0.36.41
Server Desktop Agent Version: 2.0.31.60

Project properties -> Configured Desktop Agent Version -> 2.0.31

@peterengel @Chaitanya_Priya @SAPSupport 

please help

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi @Johnny777 - please go over and click on "Join group" in the SAP Builders group with @Dan_Wroblewski - I'm sure you will get amazing knowledge there.

entry.title: Code, Create, Celebrate: Go Digital Night at SAP Headquarters
entry.content:
After a long hiatus since 2018, the Go Digital Night a learning celebration made a grand return! 

On a lovely Friday afternoon on August 2nd, the SAP Headquarters in Walldorf hosted an amazing learning party! More than 250 tech enthusiasts aged 12 and up, as well as educators, gathered for this special event, and the air was filled with curiosity and passion for continuous learning. During the event, we had international scientists, educators, and SAP teams leading workshops on various topics, from programming a microcontroller to understanding artificial intelligence or shaping an own sustainable business model or exploring SAP business software such as Success Factors and SAP Business Technology Platform. Or you could participate in crafting activities, such as using a digital embroidery machine or working with mandalas. There was something for everyone! 

As the event kicked off, with an inspirational introduction by Christiane Bauer, the founder of SAP Young Thinkers and Jadga Huegle, researcher and part of the Snap! team, everyone dived headfirst into a world of creativity and innovation. This scene was followed with a role-playing warm-up lead by Matthias Haase from the Wissensfabrik where the participants could be a part of the internet and get an overview of how data and requests are transferred.

Welcome by Christiane Bauer and Jadga Huegle | Introduction to SAP and founders | Warm-up by the Wissensfabrik

The theme "Code, create, celebrate" set the tone for the evening, guiding us through a series of awesome workshops led by enthusiastic experts. 

  • Pixel Art: In this workshop, Michael Winckler from IWR University of Heidelberg, showed participants how images were created from numbers. “We didn't even need a computer because the translation of images into numbers, called "coding," could also be understood without electronics”. 
  • Snap!GPT: In this workshop, Jens Mönig, a researcher at SAP, showed the functionality and intelligence of AI language models using Snap!, together they programmed an algorithm for generating stories, and examined how different media influenced artificial creativity. 
  • Mandalas Ojo de Dios: In this workshop, Jana Podobnik an enthusiastic SAP UX designer, created with the participants their own mandalas from wool. Experiencing in this way the cultural and spiritual significance of the "Ojo de Dios" tradition and promoted mindfulness and creative expression in the middle of digital atmosphere. 
  • Art with Snap!: In this workshop, Gerd, Volker and Kitty Luise from “Mit Rat und Tat” presented how to create fascinating artwork using the Snap! programming language through creative use of movement, color, and randomness, with the option to experience this in teams of two. 
  • Robotics: In this workshop, one of the longtime Next – Gen 4 ALL ambassador, Andreas Huppert, introduced the participants to the challenge of building and programming robots like Lego EV3, Boost, and mBot without prior knowledge. 
  • Sustainable Business Model: In this workshop, former working students who founded their own company, WÖRK.APP taught the participants about how to use the Business Model Canvas method to develop and validate a startup idea from different perspectives in small teams. 
  • BTP Diamond Game: In the SAP BTP Diamond Game, Yuri Kupriianov, a SAP University Alliance team member, invited older participants like parents to playfully experience the SAP Business Technology Platform by making strategic decisions as executives and overcoming business challenges to gain practical insights into the application of SAP BTP. 
  • MicroBlocks: In this workshop, Peter Mathijssen, the organizer of fosdem junior showed the participants how to control a computer remotely as if with a remote control. They programmed a microcontroller in MicroBlocks that interacted with their Snap! game or artwork. Through this, they discovered the world of sensors and what was possible with them. 
  • Turtlestitch: In this session, Jadga Hügle and the TurtleStitch Gang (educators who joined the event after attending Snap!Shot 2024) programmed patterns with Turtlestitch and transferred them to the real world using an embroidery machine or a cutting plotter. It was amazing! They even sewed a small Alonzo, the sweet character of Snap! 

In summary, there was a lot explore everything from programming languages like Snap! to virtual reality, coding, AI, robotics, and even how to develop sustainable business ideas. Not only our young audience was busy learning but also engaged parents, colleagues and teachers participated in workshops designed for adults. The learning opportunities were practically endless! 

Event highlights, our workshops and collaborators

But it wasn't just about the workshops – the sense of community and teamwork was just as exciting. We found ourselves sharing knowledge, collaborating, and cheering each other on. The SAP University Alliance team wants to extend our biggest thanks to our amazing collaborators from other SAP teams and our external community members: 

  • SAP Vocational Training: Together with the vocational training team we learned directly from the source about the training and study opportunities at SAP. 
  • SAP Experience Garage: At this stand, visitors had the opportunity to experience augmented reality in both "old" and "new" forms through two different examples, showcasing the advancements and changes in technology over time. 
  • Virtual Reality with SAP Success Factors: Visitors had the opportunity to immerse themselves in virtual worlds and explore how VR technology is utilized in companies, offering a glimpse into the future of digital innovation. 
  • Core Game with School 42 | Heilbronn: Here, School 42 a tuition-free, non-profit and innovative coding school offered a unique approach to education. The International Bot Game Coding Competition provided programmers of all levels with the opportunity to enhance their skills, take on challenges, and engage in discussions with fellow enthusiasts. 
  • Wissensfabrik: At this stand, participants were able to independently test the IT2school AI materials and learn through unplugged examples how machines could become artificially intelligent, such as classifying monkeys or defeating individuals in farmers' chess. 
  • TUMO Mannheim: TUMO Mannheim, a creative learning center that provides practical workshops in technology, design, and media for young people to develop their creative and technical skills in an interactive setting, also was present and showed our offers and resources with the audience. 

Thanks, big time also to all of our SAP Next- Gen 4 ALL ambassadors, Interdisciplinary Center for Scientific Computing “IWR” (University of Heidelberg), University of Berkeley, University of Warwick and our external partners. Your contribution made this event truly special, impactful and unforgettable. 

The event was filled with dynamic energy and provided a rich learning experience, as captured in these heartfelt interview highlights. 

  • "I think it was very nice how the Go Digital Night was designed. It was very appealing for both young learners and adults."  
  • "I liked that each stand had several people and that thee workshops were very individual as a result."  
  • "I really enjoyed it. It was a fun experience, and I learned something new."  
  • "The Virtual Reality workshop was really great, the (technology) is just incredible!"  
  • "I learned something new and beautiful (Mandalas Workshop)!" 
  • "I think that the Go Digital Night was great! You could decide for yourself what you wanted to learn, and I learned a lot of new things today!"  
  • “I'm thrilled to be here, because I am receiving such valuable feedback for our product from the GDN participants”. – SAP Success Factors colleague 

As the evening progressed, we took a break to refuel with some delicious pizza and recharge our energy for the rest of the event. The lively atmosphere continued with a spontaneous dance party, complete with colorful balloons bouncing around the room. It was a joyful interlude that added an extra layer of fun to an already incredible evening! 

As the night came to an end, we left with hearts full of inspiration, looking forward to the next exciting chapter of Go Digital Night. It was an unforgettable night, filled with laughter, learning, and the joy of discovery! 

Thank you to all the supporters and organizers, we would love to celebrate more learning next year. 

parts of our SAP team at the end of the event

Key resources: 

If you would like to take part next time, please check our SAP University Alliances and SAP Young Thinkers offerings on our website. 

  • SAP Young Thinkers | Who we are: link  
  • SAP University Alliances | Who we are: link 
  • SAP Young Thinkers | SAP Community: link 
  • Student opportunities at SAP: check it out here! 

If you are already in the mood of getting started on your own dive into our online learning offerings here. 

  • Student Zone: Curated learning content for students. 
  • Snap!: Fun programming language. 

Do not hesitate to reach out for any questions or to engage via youngthinkers@sap.com.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Process Automation Trail Service Error
entry.content:
I could not reproduce this.

  • What landscape are you working on?
  • I will post this somewhere that someone from the SBPA team can react to it. But note I cannot guarantee what their reaction will be, nor am I support or part of development or product management ... but I will try o advocate for you here 😺

entry.title: Re: Accounting Indicator Usage in Service Orders and Service Confirmations from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 22
entry.content:
Hello Beuch

Can I use Account indicator to determine different Accounting cost when I Trigger the Service Confirmation like Material TG11（Product），where can I Configure this？ Because I have see that the reserve order only link to Movement type 291，or if we want to determine different Account cost，how should we do？

entry.title: Unable to access mobile-service-cockpit-web for SAP-Asset-Manager - HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
entry.content:
I have created ticket under Support team:
https://sapit-home-prod-004.launchpad.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/site#unifiedticketing-Display?sap-ui-app-id-hint=51a3f75c-7f9d-4c1a-8c3e-079e8e3e1a45&/detail/7005452473/ZSER

Their advice to create a post here.

I'm trying to reach:
https://mobile-service-cockpit-web.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/cockpit/v1/org/SAP-Asset-Manager_SAMCF/space/SAM_CF/index.html#/page.home

Always getting 500 Internal Server Error. I've tried different browsers, deleting cache/cookies.

I tried another link and there is no 500 Internal Server Error. I don't have access, probably it is exepcted for this link
https://mobile-service-cockpit-web.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/cockpit/v1/org/SAP-Asset-Manager_SAP-Asset-Manager-CF/space/SAP-Asset-Manager-CF/index.html#/page.apps

I have impression that there is a problem with my access to:
https://mobile-service-cockpit-web.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/cockpit/v1/org/SAP-Asset-Manager_SAMCF/space/SAM_CF/index.html#/page.home

Please someone check my permissions

entry.title: SAP CONSULTANTS SUMMER SCHOOL 2024 🎓
entry.content:
It's time to start your professional career as a SAP SE Consultant with the possibility of obtaining the official International Certification!

Join us in the new edition of the SUMMER SCHOOL OF SAP CONSULTANTS 2024. Participate in the MASTER CLASS LIVE SAP WEEK!

from September 02 to September 08, 2024, and don't miss the opportunity to start professional career as SAP S/4HANA & SAP Analytics Cloud consultants!

Are registrations open for limited 10 Professor & 100 students?

Faculty invite only their top 10 activate students in SAP S/4HANA & SAP Analytics Cloud

They will be able to obtain an Official SAP S/4HANA & SAP Analytics Cloud Certification and many more benefits ad awards!

This Program is EXCLUSIVE for Educational Institutions that are active members of SAP University Alliances, and will be done in SAP UCC CHICO ZOOM.

To access the FREE MASTER CLASS, register with your ACADEMIC EMAIL below and take the opportunity to meet the SAP CONSULTANT CAREER PLAN and also access our AGENDA.

FREE EVENTS

➡ (10 professors lectures only) Register here to receive the FREE TTT ERP4students & ERPsim

  • Monday, September 02 from 10 AM - 12 M: Train-The-Trainers ERP4students
  • Tuesday, September 03 from 10 AM - 12 M: Train-The-Trainers ERPsim
  • Wednesdays September 04 from 10 AM - 12 M: TTT CERTIFICATIONS SAP UCC CHICO & BRANDEMBURG UNIVERSITY

➡ (100 students only) Register here for the event FREE SUMMER 2024 SCHOOL OF CONSULTANTS

  • Thursday, September 05 from 10 AM - 12 M: KICK-OFF ERP4STUDENTS.US
  • Friday, September 06 from 10 AM - 12 M: KICK-OFF SAP S/4HANA SERIOUS GAMES TOURNAMENT
  • Sunday, September 08 from 10 AM - 12 M: FINAL TOURNAMENT | SAP ANALYTICS CLOUD EXECUTIVE REPORTS |AWARD CEREMONY🏆|

PRIVATE EVENT REGISTRATION BRANDENBURG SCHOLARSHIPS (500 USD APPROX | save 10,000 USD)

➡ (faculty, students, academic members, members ASUG DUAL EDUCATION) Register here for your Official Certification (website ERP4STUDENTS of Brandenburg University of Technology Germany & California State University, SAP UCC CHICO USA).

It's time to train the NEXT GENERATION OF SAP CONSULTANTS together!

Special thanks to: @MariaCalzadaDeN @gloriasandi @isadoraroyer 

An Initiative of: ERP4STUDENTS & BRADENBURG UNIVERSITY, California State University, SAP UCC CHICO, Epistemy Press

Network & Partner Universities: ERPsim HEC Montréal

Do you have any questions? Send them to us and we'll help you. 

See you soon!

entry.title: Re: Cannot save more than 1 record in tablecontrol
entry.content: You forgot to test SY-SUBRC and raise an exception if it fails. Make sure that your internal table g_zpaymentterm_itab contains more than 1 record. It's unusual to call an update function module more than once, you should transfer all the lines at once.

entry.title: Re: How to get authenticated user information with CAP in three different ways - Directly from the U
entry.content:
Hi Alessandro.biagi ,

I am trying to follow the same steps and added the required route in xs-app.json. Our application is a UI5 HTML application that uses managed app-router, so no custom app-router config or files in the repository.

We notice the following issue: When we try to call the end point "/user-api/currentUser", the application is reaching out to the url /user-api/currentuser  and returns a 404.

 

xs-app.json
------
{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/user-api(.*)",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "sap-approuter-userapi"
    }
  ]
}
----
xs-security.json
----
{
  "xsappname": "project1",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Security profile of called application",
  "scopes": [  {
    "name": "uaa.user",
    "description": "UAA"
  },
  {
    "name": "$XSAPPNAME.View1",
    "description": "View1"
  },{
    "name": "$XSAPPNAME.View2",
    "description": "View2"
  }],
  "role-templates": [ {
    "name": "Token_Exchange",
    "description": "UAA",
    "scope-references": [
      "uaa.user"
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "CustomRole",
    "description": "generated",
    "scope-references": [
      "$XSAPPNAME.View1"
    ]
  },{
    "name": "CustomRole1",
    "description": "generated",
    "scope-references": [
      "$XSAPPNAME.View2"
    ]
  }]
}

 

Regards,

Mriganka

entry.title: PMR stuck in Queue without error
entry.content:
Hi All,
i have a problem  where i created the Production order and set the Production order for WM Material staging. Later the PMR has to be created in EWM. The PMR document is created but it is landed in Queue. But the PMR Document has no error. 
my question is why the PMR is landed in the Queue even though there is no error? 
what changes has to done to work it without getting stuck in Queue?
please guide me to solve this issue.
I really appreciate your help.
Thank you.
Regards,
VG Reddy

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Thanks Shilpa and Sheena for this month challenge.

Task 2 is completed.

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@smok1 

If you use the ADT wizard, you can specify the name of the generated transport object (R3TR TOBJ) in the "BC Management" section. In ADT you can find the generated transport object in the target package under the group "Others". By default, it has the same name as the BC maintenance object (R3TR SMBC). You can also find the name of the transport object in the generated behavior implementation class.
In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, you can edit the generated transport object in transaction SOBJ.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud - Bengaluru - 6th September, 2024
entry.content:
Hello Developer, 

We have the next upcoming in-person SAP CodeJam event coming near you at Bengaluru on 6th September, 2024. 

This is kindly being hosted by Tarento Technologies, India. We still have 10 seats available at the time of posting this discussion. Please register soon if you would like to learn more about SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP).

Register here: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/sap-cloud-application-programming-model-with-sap-hana-cloud/ev-p/13788891

entry.title: #01 - Discover the Onboarding Journey with SAP: Your Roadmap to Success
entry.content:
So, you’ve signed the contract with SAP—what’s next? Your onboarding journey has officially begun. This journey is designed to ensure a smooth and successful implementation of your SAP solution, and it’s divided into four key phases: Get Started with SAP, Plan Your Implementation, Get Enabled, and Prepare to Go Live.

The Onboarding Journey Begins

As soon as the contract is signed, the onboarding process kicks off. Within four days, your designated IT Contact Person (written in the contract) will receive a welcome email from SAP. This email containing the essential info on how to get started with the solution. Please, request this contact to verify the spam folder.

What’s Inside the Welcome Email?

This welcome email is your first step on the onboarding journey. It directs you to essential assets that will be your companions throughout the implementation:

  • Onboarding Resource Center: This is your one-stop-shop for all things onboarding. It houses a comprehensive collection of guides, checklists, and tools that will help you navigate each phase of your journey.
  • Onboarding Webinars: These webinars are designed to provide you with deep insights into the onboarding process, offering expert advice and best practices.
  • Embedded Launch Activities (EmLA) Resources: If your solution includes Embedded Launch Activities, these resources will be invaluable. They’re tailored to ensure that your launch is as seamless as possible.
  • Preferred Success Information: If you’ve purchased Preferred Success, this email will also provide details on how to access this premium support service, which is designed to accelerate your SAP journey.

Note: It’s important to distinguish this Welcome Email from the Provisioning Email. The Provisioning Email, which you’ll receive later, contains critical system information, whereas the Welcome Email focuses on resources to help you get started.

What Should You Do Next?

From the moment the contract is signed until it officially starts, it’s crucial to familiarize yourself with the content of the onboarding journey. Here’s what we recommend:

  1. Explore the Onboarding Resource Center: Take the time to browse through the resources available. This will give you a solid understanding of what to expect and how to prepare for each phase.
  2. Incorporate the Four Phases into Your Project Timeline: Use the assets provided to map out the four phases of the onboarding journey within your project timeline. This will ensure that your team is aligned and ready for each stage.
  3. Register for the Onboarding Webinars: These sessions are invaluable for getting up to speed with the onboarding process. Make sure to plan, register, and join these sessions to gain insights and clarify any questions you might have.

Need an Implementation Partner?

If you’re still looking for an implementation partner, SAP provides a helpful resource at Find SAP Partners. This page helps you understand the benefits of working with an SAP partner, how to select the right one, and how to locate potential partners using SAP Partner Finder. Choosing the right partner can make a significant difference in the success of your implementation.

In Conclusion

The onboarding journey with SAP is meticulously designed to ensure that you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation. By following the steps outlined in the welcome email and leveraging the resources provided, you’ll set a strong foundation for your project. Remember, this journey is not just about getting started—it’s about ensuring long-term success with your SAP solution. So take the time to explore, plan, and engage with the resources at your disposal. The road to a successful go-live starts here.

entry.title: Migration of Workflow to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My team and I are currently engaged in migrating workflows from Workflow Management to SAP Build Process Automation (BPA). We have been following the guidelines outlined in the documentation available at SAP Help Portal. However, we encountered an issue during step 4, which requires the import of the MTAR file from the old workflow, as there was no content available in the Manage Package application. Upon attempting this, we received the following error:

Additionally, it is important to note that our workflow module does not contain any "*.extension" files or any "packageinfo".

We seek your guidance on resolving this issue and would appreciate confirmation on whether our current approach is appropriate.

Furthermore, we plan to utilize BPA exclusively for workflow development. Could you please advise if it is feasible to edit the workflows directly within BPA after importing them, or will we need to continue using BAS for older workflows?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Paul

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung, the game board does not work yet for 2024, right?

It's showing that my ID is not yet registered (in german).

entry.title: Restrict BOM creation in a plant but allow plant assignment using CS07
entry.content:
I want to be able to restrict the creation of BOMs in 2 plants. lets say 1001 and 1002. But I should be able to assign group BOMs to these plants using CS07.

I have looked at the below Q&A and tried authorization object C_STUE_WRK, but this restricts both CS01 and CS07.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/cs01-restriction-on-plant/qaq-p/8772905

I would appreciate the help.

entry.title: Integration of Microsoft Teams into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Cummunity,

My goal is to integrate Microsoft Teams with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

As a first step, I managed to get both the “As Chat” and “As Card” functions up and running.

In the case of “As Tab”, an error message appears during the process:

 

Can anyone help me with this? Is this a case for my Teams administrator?

I'm looking forward to your feedback.

Best regards

Sebastian

entry.title: CAP Application getting 404 after deployment to CF
entry.content:
Hi all,

I was following this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/group.deploy-full-stack-cap-application.html

After deploying the application to cloud foundry environment, which worked fine so well, I cant open the application. What I get is an: 

Cannot GET /

Do you guys have any idea why this happens?

entry.title: Migrating Workflows to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My team and I are currently developing an application that requires the integration of workflows. It has come to my attention that SAP is actively addressing this topic, with an update released on August 16th.

At present, we are encountering issues when attempting to import these workflows into SAP Build Process Automation (BPA). I came across a community post from a year ago in which an SAP expert mentioned that workflows developed within BAS could only be managed and updated within BAS. Our intention was to use BPA exclusively for development.

Given the recent update, we would appreciate clarification on whether it is now possible to use BPA as the sole development environment, or if we are still required to manage the older workflows in BAS.

For reference, here is the community post from a year ago:
Workflow Migration 

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,

Gabriel

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - Security - OAuth 2.0 - Understanding Token Exchange
entry.content:
Hi Carlos, thanks for this amazing blog post. 

Unfortunately my team is facing a bit of a problem. Our frontend (FLP) doesn't have the "$XSAPPNAME(application,backendxsuaa).backendscope" scope in our JWT token. Thus all calls to our backend apis are returning a 403. Do you perhaps know what may be the cause? 

We have a similar architecture to what you've shown us, minus the core application on the frontend. Our frontend is deployed on CF while our backend is deployed on Kyma.

Thank you 🙂

For those who might be able to help you, it might help to clarify what landscape you are on and what you meant by "being moved to the platform".

Hello,
I'm not able to reproduce the issue you're experiencing. Could you please share your tenant link? Additionally, does this issue occur after you create a new project? What happens when you open an existing project?

entry.title: Multiple items of one material in a delivery an QM Inspection lot creation
entry.content:
Hi,

supplier sends single material as multiple items in a delviery, and later those multiple items are posted in MIGO. The problem is realted to SKIP Inspection lots. 

QM inspection lot origin 01 is active. In insp type setup field "Control InsLot" I can select:

"For each material document item" - standard setup that will create multiple inspection lots, not what I want. Good thing is that for SKIP lots, it is posted to unrestricted stock directly.

"1 For each material document, material, batch and storage location" - that creates one inspection lot, which is what I wanted, but my problem is that for SKIP lots material is posted to Q stock. 

due to QM and WM intefrace setup I don't want to post SKIP lots to Q stock but this is SAP's standard:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002077450

How can I ovecome this ? To have "1 For each material document, material, batch and storage location" but post to unrestricted.

entry.content: Hello Shahbhat,

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome!

entry.title: Re: How to Reduce Database DTU on SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Great write up, Yannick, very useful for us.

I note that you ignore 'Used (max)' for the analysis, but do you know if this metric is used by SAP/Azure when calculating DTU usage.

entry.title: Decomissioning SAP ECC6.0 and safeguarding archived data in cloud storage
entry.content: Our customer is currently implementing S/4HANA public cloud and wants to decommission the existing SAP ECC 6.0 legacy system afterwards. Before decommissioning, they need to find a solution (that fits well to the new S/4HANA Public Cloud solution), so that they can still access historic archived data for authorities and tax purposes (10 years back). They are considering migrating archived data to a new cloud storage. Would you have any recommendations how to best archive that (tooling and process)?

entry.title: Re: Data Binding with Repeating Items and Complex Tables in Print Forms
entry.content:
Hi @yoppie_ariesthio,

I have seen this in the past and tried many different things to get rid of this effect, but I couldn't find a comprehensive solution yet. It seems to have something to do with the table setup. If I remember correctly, I have never seen this with a "normal" table -> when you only have a single body row.

Some root cause criteria seem to be:

- this "body row + details subform" section combination
- after a page break
- with "Include header row in subsequent pages" enabled

Two unsatisfying workarounds can be:
- Disable "Include header row in subsequent pages"
- Repeat the whole table (instead of only body rows) -> then you have the header row above every item

I will use this request to look into this topic again, but it will take some time. I'll give an update if there are any news.

Hope that helps!

Best regards,
Felix

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Self-Service Requisitioning - Internal Procurement Catalogs

entry.title: Re: Generative AI with SAP – Part 2: Components of SAP Business AI
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: EWM bin sorting
entry.content:
Hello,

There are two ways that we can sort according to your query: 

1. If you maintain Bin Master Data (eg. Aisle, Stack, Level), you can configure Define Sort Sequence for Activity Areas. You will be able to sort by those criteria

2. If you did not maintain or just want to sort by bin name just maintain only storage type in Define Sort Sequence for Activity Areas and everything else leave it as blank you will be able to achieve that.

Br,

Sorawit

I'm trying to establish connection for ERP with SAP Integration Suite. Just wanted to know which version of ERP will be capable of connecting with the Integration Suite.

Thank You

Aditya

entry.title: External settlements missing in PS hierarchy reports for internal orders related to WBS-Elements
entry.content:
While running a standard PS hierarchy report e.g. S_ALR_87013531, external settlement postings for internal orders are not shown. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: External settlements missing in PS hierarchy reports...
entry.content:
The reported behaviour is not an error but standard design. This is documented in KBA 2733265 - External settlements (KOAE) not updated to RPSCO.

More detailed information can be found in the referenced Consulting Note 51971 - Hierarchy reports: Displaying actual costs after settlement.

entry.title: Is it possible to automatically print an Object Link Document upon release of a production order
entry.content:
We have an external application running a production spec and document system that uses Supabase that is unsupportable and no longer works with our version of SAP after our migration away from ECC to S4 Hana Public Cloud

To replace this system I am looking to introduce the document management to store and publish production specs. I have created some object links to materials and added them to the BOMs

Is there a way to have copies of these specs print out automatically with production paperwork and picklist upon production order release?

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud UM: Transpose your data
entry.content:
Hello dear reader! In the world of financial and business modeling, the ability to effectively organize and manipulate data is crucial. If you are searching for the way how to do it in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Universal Model (SAP PaPM UM) we can tell you that system offers a function that can help with that. The Transfer Structure function is the feature for it.

Transfer Structure allows users to transpose and pivot data based on predefined conditions and settings, making it easier to analyze and interpret complex datasets. This function is particularly valuable for creating detailed financial reports and business models.

Let’s see how you can create this function.

For instance, we will utilize the data provided in the table below that will be part of Input function - a Model Entity that encompasses data on expenses tracked over two regions for various cost centers. Our goal is to “restructure” this table to the one that will have separate columns for each cost center which will represent a form that would meet the requirements of financial cost management. You can see which values went to which columns by paying attention to colors in the table.

Please take a look at the that will be restructured:

 Below check the result table given by Transfer Structure:


 (1) Create and configure

Creation of the Transfer Structure should start in the Manage Functions screen, where you can choose a Type and maintain Function ID and Description. From this screen, you can go to the specific screen for Transfer Structure where the needed configuration can be set.

In order to configure Transfer Structure function, you can choose Edit button on top right corner.

Once you're in the editing mode, you can navigate to the General Information section. Here, you will find a variety of options that allow you to arrange the specific details you want to see in the function's results.

After Environment ID, Function ID and Type, you will find an Input where you can maintain function that has data that you want to use for transposing, in our case that will be Model Entity with data from the first table provided above.

Additionally, the General Information section offers options below about which you can read more in the

  • Enriched Result Handling – Include Enriched Data
  • Suppress initial Results - Yes
  • Ensure distinct Results - Yes
  • Rule Result Field - RRFIeld.

Stay tuned as we reveal how you can harness their power with a practical example in the continuation of this blog post!

(2) Rules section

A core part of the Transfer Structure is the Rules section. That is the place where you can define how values should be transposed. The gist is that this function works as IF-THEN statement in term that in the Condition section user should set IF part with the possibility to go more into details by using selections. An Assignment section is considered as THEN part and it specified from which field’s values should be transposed(in the system marked as Source Value Field) and to which field they will be transposed(in the system marked as Field).

In the first rule, we want to define that expenses for “Development” cost center should be shown in the Development_Expenses column.

Since we have 4 different cost centers, we will create separate rules with the same approach as shown, in which we will determine what should happen to the values for that specific cost center. As a plus, we will add one more rule that will help in showing the difference between flavors in configuration. In that rule, we say that for “Development” cost center tracked in APAC area expenses should be written down in HR_Expenses field.

(3) Results

When you finish with the creation of Transfer Structure, go to the Manage Environments screen to activate the environment where the function is located and then go to the Manage Functions screen in order to Run the function. Choose the semantic link Show Data to open a screen where you can check results.

For Enriched Results Handling we chose “Include Enriched Data” which means that only enriched data will be present in the result. Also, if you remember from the Input table there was a row in which we didn’t have value for expenses for the Human Resources cost center in the EMEA region.

In the General Information section we checked the option Suppress Initial Results and that is why mentioned row was excluded from the result.

We selected Ensure Distinct Results, causing Rule5 to be ignored due to overlapping conditions with Rule1. Both rules apply to the “Development” cost center, and since the system processed Rule1 first, subsequent rule with the same selection was disregarded.

Please take note that zeroes will fill the places not matching the Conditions since zero is number that can’t impact negatively results if the Transfer Structure will be forwarded as an Input for doing some calculations.

Let’s now change the configuration a bit. We will copy the previously created Transfer Structure, enter the edit mode and uncheck Suppress Initial Results and Ensure Distinct Results. When changes are saved, remember to activate the environment and execute function.

 New result is:

You can now observe a result that contrasts with the initial one. This time, the row with no recorded expenses for the "Human Resources(HR)" cost center in EMEA is included. Additionally, from the Rule result field, which indicates the applied rule for each row, it is evident that Rule5 has been utilized. Consequently, the expense for the "Development" cost center from APAC region is now also reflected in the HR Expenses column.

(4) Include All Data

Let's explore another scenario. There's one more option in the Enriched Result Handling drop-down menu called "Include All Data." We'll take the previously modified function and make further adjustments by copying it first. In this scenario, we'll not only update the Enriched Result Handling but also delete Rule4, which manages the “Human Resources(HR)” cost center, as well as Rule5.

After successful activation and execution, you will be able to open Show Data screen that will represent you the result:

If you can remember the configuration of rules R1, R2 and R3, they are handling expenses for “Development”, “Maintenance” and “Marketing”, while Rule R4 was responsible for the "Human Resources(HR)" cost center, but with removal of it, expenses for "Human Resources(HR)" would typically not be enriched. However, due to the "Include All Data" option being selected, we still see the data for the " Human Resources(HR)" cost center with zeros for all new fields introduced with Transfer Structure because zero is a neutral number.

In summary, the Transfer Structure function empowers users to efficiently transpose and pivot their data, streamlining the analysis of intricate datasets. You can use what you learned here to embrace the capability to transform your data handling and elevate your analytical processes to new heights.

Thank you for exploring the potential of the Transfer Structure function with us. Stay tuned for new interesting features in the future! Happy day!

entry.title: Re: Approval Workflow for Service Quotation
entry.content:
Hi Sanju

this seems like a rather complicated process for a simple approval step. When will SAP provide a solution for Service quotations or Service contracts (on privat cloud) using the Flexible workflow mechanism like it is available for e.g. sales document (F3014 app).

Thank you

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Join the "SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors" Adoption Lab
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have updated the registration page. Recheck it!

Provide the right tenant ID for the question : Please provide the Tenant ID where Joule will be activated?

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2408
entry.content:
SAUG, our local user group, love to throw us a challenge and they managed just that by booking their main conference event on a Mon/Tues once again.  That leaves us with a Monday night Stammtisch and Mondays are pretty much the hardest night to get people to come out

It was the same deal last year and, although the venue worked great (Harujuku Gyoza), the bar closed a little too early and we had to find somewhere else to go. Most places in Sydney close pretty early on a Monday. We resolved to get the bar to delay last orders for us

With decades of SAP project experience between us, you wouldn't think that @jeff_t  and I would make such a rookie mistake, but we ended up speaking to the wrong manager at the bar.  We spoke to the events manager in the weeks leading up to the night, but when we got to the bar the onsite manager hadn't been briefed and had to call in extra staff.  Luckily we had a really good turnout once again and we made it well worth their while

As usual in the lead up to the event I heard from lots of Stammtisch regulars that regrettably they wouldn't make it this time and, as usual, this made me anxious. I needn't have worried because there were lots of folks who could and did make it.  Amongst those were some new faces which is always great to see

Much kudos to those people who brought a friend along or even many friends or colleagues

Our gathering burnt very brightly for a while - I would say that once again we had about 35 friends together at the peak. The conference attendees were in the minority I would say, but I do think it is important for us to welcome the visitors, including some from overseas. Before 9pm things started winding down and so we didn't really need that late bar after all! Our new friend the manager didn't mind because the extra staff had been busy enough and he had pretty decent takings for a Monday night

Once again I'm left to reflect on the great SAP community we have here in Sydney. Even on a Monday night in winter we had a great turnout and it was a joy to see people meeting new friends and catching up with old ones.

Please let us know what you thought of the event. Likely the next one will be in November, before the Christmas party madness, so venue suggestions will be gratefully received

Here are my photos:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

And from @jeff_t

entry.title: Re: Secure Authentication Strategy for HTTP Sender Channels in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi @michael_schmidt ,

Thank you for your question.

If you are using SAP Cloud Integration APIs then there is no cost involved if both consumer & producer are SAP solution.

As per the above solution, if you are using SAP API management on one side and SAP Cloud Integration on the other then metrics in SAP Cloud Integration depends on receiver system (or where you are sending data after receiving it from SAP APIM).

BR
Adarsh

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )

I get the following warning though for the line _Status._Text.Text as StatusText: 
The association "_Text" can modify the cardinality of the result set ZENTITY_SHAL_TRAVEL (Data Definition) /TRL_EN/.adt/ddic/ddlsources/zentity_shal_travel line 17 ABAP Syntax Check Problem

The CDS Views for routing related data can be found via the following resources:

1. Manufacturing Bill of Operations | SAP Help Portal

2. Use Fiori App "Customer Data Browser" or "View Browser" by using the keyword " manufacturing bill of operations" as below:

Best regards,

Radu

entry.title: How to use Content-ID to reference the ETag in an OData Batch Request?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm trying to send a batch request following the OData V4 Guideline: https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_ReferencinganETag

My request body and the response is:

In my batch request, I tried to read an entity with Content-ID:1 and use this Content-ID as the referenced Etag in the If-Match part for the PATCH request. The response said my second request's syntax is wrong.

entry.title: Re: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2408
entry.content: It's so nice to see everyone in jackets!  It's still summer here and hot and humid.

entry.title: Re: EWM authorization control stock type
entry.content: I also have the same requirement, can you let me know if have find any solution to this.

entry.title: Adding a new field in v2 oDataModel binded to a sap.ui.table.Table / TreeTable in UI5 app.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone

I have an oData EntitySet "CUSTOMER_HIER_LIST" directly binded to sap.ui.table.TreeTable using v2 oDataModel and displays the treetable rows using treeAnnotationProperties binding. Now I want to initialize a new field called "IS_SELECTED" for each row. I have a sap.m.CheckBox in each row to which I will bind it. This field is not present in the CUSTOMER_HIERARCHY_LIST entity's metadata. I am not able to use Table.addSelectionRows() as I have a custom requirement for row selection.

In the 'dataReceived' event of the tree binding, if I try to do like below, then I see the error in the browser console as "Assertion failed: IS_SELECTED is not a valid property path" which is true because the field is not present in the entity set.

 

onCustomerListDataRecieved: function(oEvent){
  const oTreeTable = this.getView().byId("treeTableId"),
  aBindingContexts = oTreeTable.getBinding("rows").getContexts();
  aBindingContexts.forEach((oRowContext)=>{
     //This works because the field is present in entityset
     this.getModel("odataV2").setProperty("CUST_DESCR", "TEST CUSTOMER", oRowContext);
     //This does not work as the field is not present in entityset's metdata
     this.getModel("odataV2").setProperty("IS_SELECTED", false, oRowContext);
  });
}

 

I can change the value of an existing field, but then is it even possible to add a new field to v2 oDataModel as above?

Any suggestions and solutions will be greatly appreciated. 

Thanks,

Naresh

entry.title: Debit credit Mirroring
entry.content:
Hello all,

We need to setup a new process to initiate debit to transporter through mirroring. Whenever a credit note was issue to customer for shortage of damage, transporter which is there in the shipment should get debited automatically.

Is there any job or any other process that we need to follow to create this request. Please help.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Upcoming Expert Deep Dive Live Sessions for Scripting Support , (Cloud) Hotfolders and Interceptors
entry.content:
I am excited to announce that I will be conducting live training sessions next week on the 3rd,4th and 5th of September. The sessions will cover important topics in the field of Commerce Domain.

The first session will cover the topic of Scripting Support in SAP Commerce Cloud. This 2-hour session will provide an overview of scripting support and how to apply scripts in various aspects of SAP Commerce Cloud such as ImpEx, Cronjobs, Event handling, Task engine, and dynamic Business Process management.

Registration link :Identifying Scripting Support in SAP Commerce Cloud

The second session will focus on Understanding Hot Folders and Cloud Hot Folders in SAP Commerce Cloud. This 1-hour session will cover the implementation, configuration, and customization of the “Hot Folders” functionality, as well as provide an overview of “Cloud Hot Folders” and its architecture, processing flow, and configuration with SAP Commerce Cloud.

Registration link: Understanding Hot Folders and Cloud Hot Folders in SAP Commerce Cloud 

Lastly, the third session will introduce Interceptors in SAP Commerce Cloud. This 1-hour session will cover the concept of interceptors, different interceptor types, how to implement a new interceptor, and best practices for implementation. The session will end with a practical exercise demonstrating the steps of implementing a new interceptor and testing it on the SAP Commerce Cloud Storefront.

Registration link: Introducing Interceptors in SAP Commerce Cloud 

I look forward to welcoming eager learners to these live sessions. Happy Learning!

entry.title: Re: How to activate a transport request selection bar for the RAP BO of a customizing table

If you still want to have such a feature, you need to request it via Customer Influence Portal.

best regards,
Christian

OR

Have the ability to use a script to set a custom field checkbox. Any idea how to do any of these?

entry.title: sending email after creating the notification automatically in IW28
entry.content:
Hi

We use iw28 transaction to see our work order. Is it possible to receive an email when a new work order has been created.

if it's possible I need to send automatically email for each work center a separate email 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Thanks! This could potentially be a very useful functionallity. I have a question, how can you filter the data on the graph? This is important, to replicate same graph but showing different data (eg. countries).

In the SAC story, the graphs are all filtered via page input control.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Hanumath,

The below BOTs are available for usage in SAP Business ByDesign. 

Sales Order Creation from Local Purchase Order (1C1)
Automated stock transfer order (2S2)
Business Document Extraction from E-Mail (1x1)
Sales Order Creation from Local Purchase Order (1C1) (Depreceated)
Automated supplier invoice upload (1R1)
Account Master Data Enrichment for Document Information Extraction (1C2) (Depreceated)
Automate GR/IR Clearing
WIP Clearing (SAP Business ByDesign)
Proof of Delivery Note Upload in the Outbound Delivery and the Invoice (1S1)
Schedule Report Refresh (1X2)
Automated Purchase Order Upload (2R2)

Currently Projects are not available for BPA usage. Hope this is clear.

Best Regards,
Arun

entry.title: Re: Where to find SAP wide Forum for AI SAP specific topics?
entry.content:
Hi @MaximilianPurk that is a great question I have gone through as well.

Closed source like OpenAI, Google or Anthropic apart, the AI tooling is thrilling ecosystem in the open source community, mostly happening on Discord, Github and Hugging Face.

I already assumed that for us, in the SAP ecosystem, this is the forum of discussion, but if the tool is a Langchain tool connecting to SAP, the langchain forums will be a good place, while this forum is the right place for the BTP builds and the nature of SAP will make it natural to communicate here. I still see the gen AI open source community some way of immature for enterprise ready applications and discussions.

That is my reasoning, let me know what you think

entry.title: Re: SFC Card plugin extension in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hello @ksinghal,
Have you tried clearing cache and using incognito mode?
If you don´t see the extension, I suggest you review your whole setup, perhaps start fresh and follow the steps from this blog post strictly, and it should work.
Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: how to enable Auto release via 'MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2'
entry.content: i've try to used MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2 for Service Entry Sheets. Already put the filter correctly but somehow it doesn't work. i put external breakpoint with SAP_WRFT but nothing happen

entry.title: SAP RAP - Determination of on modify is not triggered during change
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

currently I´m trying to implement a determination with on modify into my RAP Application. So far so good, but the defined ABAP Method is only called while pressing save.

As far as I´ve understood the documentation and blog post, "on modify" will instantly being triggered, as soon as the definition is fulfilled. The handling is happening in the transactional buffer - So it should work without pressing save, right?

 

 

  determination detCreateValues on modify { create; }
  determination onChangeEQUNR on modify { field equnr; }

 

 

I´ve also implemented SideEffects in my UI for the field, but the ABAP Method is still not triggered

Can somehelp give me a solution for it? Maybe I understood something wrong


Notes:
HANA2020 Release
Non Draft Application

entry.title: Re: Adding a new field in v2 oDataModel binded to a sap.u...
entry.content:
Unfortunately, in the case of OData binding, my suggestion is to add the attribute to the CUSTOMER_HIER_LIST. You can create a dummy attribute (if it's in a CDS, it might look like this):
 

cast ( '' as BOOLE_D ) as IS_SELECTED

This should solve your issue.

Best regards,
Sebastiano

entry.title: Re: Push notifications badge
entry.content:
Thanks for replying Dan.
Have you ever successfully implemented push notifications on one of your apps?

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
My demo system has no entries in standard table /dmo/oall_stat which is used by view /DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH. Hence , couldn't fetch text value.

1. No entries in /dmo/oall_stat

2. Data preview for task 2

3. CDS definition

entry.title: Re: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2408
entry.content:
A great evening. It takes everyone who came along to join in & network, which they did!

Mike and I get a lot of pleasure from curating an atmosphere for our Sydney Stammtisch to flourish. And we enjoy meeting you!

entry.title: Re: SAP provided official document type list
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for your question.

You may find the list of SAP delivered standard document types for Purchase orders in SSCUI 102909 Define Document Types.

When you create a new purchase order, the system inserts the document type NB by default. You can change the document type of the purchase order and use any customer-defined document type (Z*) that has been copied with all dependent entries from document type NB.

NB stands for the document type used for standard purchase orders.

Please refer to the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3051509

Best regards,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @stefan_walz ,

Great Blog!! May I have questions for OP 2023 

Is event based revenue recognition is mandatory? If not, how to recognize revenue?

If item based accounting is not activated? how does the system settle the cost?

Thanks

Jane

Vijayendra

Eduardo

entry.title: Re: Sessions TechEd Virtual VS Live
entry.content:
Nice video! 😁

Yes, I’ve already selected as many catalog sessions as I could.
My favorites are the AI sessions that allow for extending functionality while keeping the core clean, and the "jump-start" sessions where there are tutorials and hands-on.

As a learning opportunity, I started with the SAP Business AI Portfolio learning journey.
However, I paused it because I’m writing an SAP Press Book, and I might wait until after TechEd in case something new comes out.

I hope to participate on SAP TechEd on tour and the session are published as soon as possible there as well 👀

entry.title: SAP the Date: 23th SAP Stammtisch Ostschweiz am 10.9.2024
entry.content:
Am SAP Stammtisch Ostschweiz/Sanktgallen treffen sich periodisch SAPinteressierte Personen aus Beratungshäusern, Freelancer und von Kunden und zelebrieren after work culture.

Dabei darf auch über SAP und die damit erlebten Erfahrungen im Geschäftsalltag gesprochen werden.

Natürlich gibt es auch immer tagesaktuelle Themen aus Politik, Gesellschaft und Kulturellem was Diskussionen anregt.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content: None of the 2024 Devtoberfest badges have been assigned yet.  So although you are registered for the group, the gameboard can't see that yet because you don't have the badge.  We will start the badge assignments closer to the start of the event itself.

entry.title: Re: My geekiest hobby is tracking tagged ocean sharks—it’s the perfect way to clear my mind and refo
entry.content:
This hobby is awesome! 😄

Do you have a favorite shark?

entry.title: Re: Getting 500 Error when Trying to execute POST request...
entry.content:
Hello Ron,

Sorry for experiencing such an issue. What I was able to find is that the authentication request succeeds, but the main request returns 500.
Since we do not have very helpful logs for the main request, I am looking at the REST API documentation and you can try the following:

- If not already, double check the headers in the request - if the 'Content-Type': 'application/json' or 'Accept': 'application/json' or both headers should be used (or any other).
- Try setting the timeout of the http request from the default 3 seconds to 10 seconds.
- Since it is a POST request, the body parameter could be problematic. Try to escape special characters if there are any (using dynamic expressions like toEscapedJson/toUrlEncoded/toPercentEncoded)

I hope these suggestions prove useful
